note: Responsive Tables Filter ships a single core text-format filter plugin (id filter_responsive_tables_filter, 'Apply responsive behavior to HTML tables.', TYPE_TRANSFORM_REVERSIBLE) plus an optional module settings form for Views/theme tables. The filter has two per-format settings: tablesaw_type (stack|columntoggle|swipe, default stack) and tablesaw_persist (boolean, default TRUE). When enabled on a text format the state lives in the core filter.format.<id> config entity under filters.filter_responsive_tables_filter (status + settings). Medium/hard cases are grounded in that real config entity: introspection reads the filter's presence/mode/persist on a format; execution enables the filter / sets its mode and verifies the live config. Filters only transform tables that have a <thead>, skip tables carrying a no-tablesaw class, and allow per-table mode override via a tablesaw-<mode> class.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal contrib module responsive_tables_filter, what is the machine id of the text-format filter plugin it provides, and what plugin type / filter processing type is it?
evals.expected_output: The filter plugin id is `filter_responsive_tables_filter` (title 'Apply responsive behavior to HTML tables.'), a core filter plugin of type TYPE_TRANSFORM_REVERSIBLE provided by the responsive_tables_filter module.

evals.prompt: What per-text-format settings does the responsive_tables_filter filter plugin expose, what are their allowed values, and what are their defaults?
evals.expected_output: Two settings: tablesaw_type (the default Tablesaw mode: stack, columntoggle, or swipe; default stack) and tablesaw_persist (boolean, keep the first column persistently visible; default TRUE).

evals.prompt: How does the responsive_tables_filter filter make an HTML table responsive? Name what it adds to the table markup, the one structural requirement a table must meet to be processed, and how an editor can opt a single table out or override the mode.
evals.expected_output: It adds the classes `tablesaw` and `tablesaw-<mode>` plus data-tablesaw-mode / data-tablesaw-minimap attributes to each <table>, marks each <th> with role=columnheader (and priority/persist data attributes), and attaches the responsive_tables_filter/tablesaw-filter (Tablesaw) library. The table must have a <thead> or it is skipped. An editor opts a table out with a `no-tablesaw` class, and overrides the mode on one table by adding a `tablesaw-stack`/`tablesaw-columntoggle`/`tablesaw-swipe` class.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site the Responsive Tables Filter is enabled on exactly one text format. Which text format is it, and which Tablesaw display mode (stack, columntoggle, or swipe) is configured as its default? Inspect the live text-format (filter_format) configuration to answer.
evals.expected_output: The filter_responsive_tables_filter filter is enabled on the Full HTML format (full_html) with tablesaw_type = swipe. It is stored in filter.format.full_html under filters.filter_responsive_tables_filter (status: true).

evals.prompt: The Responsive Tables Filter has been enabled on the Basic HTML text format on this site. Is its 'persistent first column' option (tablesaw_persist) turned on or off for that format? Inspect the live filter_format configuration for Basic HTML to answer.
evals.expected_output: It is OFF: in filter.format.basic_html the filter_responsive_tables_filter instance has settings.tablesaw_persist = false (with tablesaw_type = stack).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, enable the Responsive Tables Filter (filter plugin filter_responsive_tables_filter) on the Full HTML text format so that tables authored in Full HTML fields get responsive Tablesaw behavior. Apply the change to the live site.
evals.expected_output: In filter.format.full_html the filters map contains filter_responsive_tables_filter with status: true, so the responsive tables filter runs on Full HTML output.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site the Responsive Tables Filter is already enabled on the Basic HTML text format but using the default stack mode. Change its default Tablesaw display mode to Column Toggle (columntoggle) for the Basic HTML format, leaving the filter enabled. Apply the change to the live site.
evals.expected_output: In filter.format.basic_html the filter_responsive_tables_filter instance stays enabled (status: true) and its settings.tablesaw_type is changed to columntoggle.
